context: Agent was given a phased decommission plan where a naive topological sort would place infrastructure cleanup (Phase 1) in a different wave from the service code cleanup (Phases 2 & 3). The critical correctness test is whether the agent recognises that deploying Phase 1 alone would break the live services at runtime, and therefore groups Phases 1, 2, and 3 into the same must-land-together wave.

checklist.name: phases-1-2-3-same-wave
checklist.description: Phases 1, 2, and 3 are placed in the same wave (not in separate waves). Placing Phase 1 in an earlier wave and Phases 2/3 in a later wave is a critical failure.

checklist.name: must-land-together-label
checklist.description: The wave containing Phases 1, 2, and 3 is labelled 'must land together' (or equivalent) with an explicit note that deploying Phase 1 alone would break the live services at runtime.

checklist.name: atomic-deploy-note
checklist.description: The wave document includes a blockquote or note stating that the infrastructure change (Phase 1) and the service code changes (Phases 2 & 3) must be deployed in the same operation.

checklist.name: phase-5-after-phases-2-3
checklist.description: Phase 5 (remove SDK package dependency) is placed after Phases 2 and 3, not before or alongside them, since removing the package before the import is deleted would break the services.

checklist.name: phase-0-first-wave
checklist.description: Phase 0 (validation) is the sole occupant of Wave 1 and is labelled sequential or pre-work.

checklist.name: phases-2-3-parallel-within-wave
checklist.description: Phases 2 and 3 are noted as independently workable (parallel worktrees) within their wave, since they touch different files.

checklist.name: verification-checklists-present
checklist.description: Each wave has a Verification: checklist with at least one concrete, runnable command
